objective: Submit the first successful Kaggle submission for the NVIDIA Nemotron Model Reasoning Challenge, then use that accepted path as the base for higher-score LoRA and workflow improvements.

userStories.title: Freeze the official competition constraints in repo docs
acceptanceCriteria: The repo captures official competition overview, evaluation, data, discussion, and rules text under docs/competition
The submission contract no longer assumes a CSV file
Remaining unknowns are listed explicitly instead of guessed
userStories.notes: This is already completed and should be treated as frozen context for the first submission path.

userStories.title: Validate the submission demo notebook as the first-submission path
acceptanceCriteria: The preferred first-submit path is explicitly notebooks/nvidia-nemotron-submission-demo.ipynb
The notebook path is aligned with the captured submission contract: submission.zip, compatible LoRA adapter, adapter_config.json, rank <= 32
Any unresolved runtime or packaging assumptions are written down before spending more submissions
userStories.notes: Keep this story focused on validating the first-submit path, not on raising score yet.

userStories.title: Produce the first Kaggle-ready submission.zip artifact
acceptanceCriteria: The submission demo path produces a submission.zip artifact
The artifact location and any run notes are recorded in progress.txt
checks: Verify the packaging summary from the notebook run
userStories.notes: This story ends when the artifact is ready for Kaggle submission.

userStories.title: Record the first accepted Kaggle submission result
acceptanceCriteria: The user confirms Kaggle accepted the submission artifact
The acceptance status and any public score are logged in progress.txt
The next score-improvement direction is named clearly
checks: Manual Kaggle submission confirmation from the user
userStories.notes: This story requires the actual Kaggle submit action and confirmation from the competition UI.

userStories.title: Create the first post-submit improvement plan
acceptanceCriteria: The repo has a clear next-step task list for score improvement
The first improvement phase stays within the official rules and evaluation assumptions
The plan distinguishes between deterministic hardening, LoRA quality improvements, and broader experiment ideas
checks: Review the updated prd.json or a successor PRD
userStories.notes: Do not start broad score work until the first submission path is proven.
